title: Explorers' Toolkit
description: New biomes, hidden structures, and smoother performance in a simple, exploration-focused package. Forge 1.20.1!
body:
# 🪓 Explorers' Toolkit

## Exploration-focused world generation

The main goal of this modpack is to make exploration more meaningful. The world is filled with new places to discover: temples hidden in the forest, expanded cave systems, ancient ruins beneath the surface, detailed villages, strange monuments in the sea, and custom-built dungeons scattered around the map.

These additions are designed to blend naturally into the world, encouraging exploration without making things feel too chaotic or cluttered. You can still recognize your surroundings — there’s just a lot more waiting to be found.

## Lightweight and optimized
Mods like Embeddium, Rubidium Eats, and other backend optimizations are included to improve FPS and reduce lag, especially during exploration or when loading complex structures.

The pack avoids heavy overhauls and large mod systems to keep load times fast and performance stable, even on mid-range hardware. If you’ve had trouble running large modpacks before, this one aims to stay accessible.

## Vanilla-friendly balance

Despite the extra content, the game still plays like Minecraft. There are no complex RPG systems, massive tech trees, or drastic gameplay changes. Instead, this pack focuses on expanding what’s already there.

It’s easy to jump in and play — no wiki reading or tutorials required. The mods are chosen to feel like natural extensions of the base game, making it perfect for casual survival players, builders, and explorers alike.
